T1003.001.name: OS Credential Dumping
T1003.001.description: Adversaries may attempt to access credential material stored in the process memory of the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS).

T1059.001.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.001.description: Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.

T1053.005.name: Scheduled Task/Job
T1053.005.description: Adversaries may abuse the Windows Task Scheduler to perform task scheduling for initial or recurring execution of malicious code.

T1547.001.name: Boot or Logon Autostart Execution
T1547.001.description: Adversaries may achieve persistence by adding a program to a startup folder or referencing it with a Registry run key.

T1543.003.name: Create or Modify System Process
T1543.003.description: Adversaries may create or modify Windows services to repeatedly execute malicious payloads as part of persistence.

T1135.name: Network Share Discovery
T1135.description: Adversaries may look for folders and drives shared on remote systems as a precursor to lateral movement or collection.
